interpretation: The PTAFR gene encodes the platelet-activating factor receptor, which is a G-protein coupled receptor that mediates various biological responses, including inflammation and smooth muscle contraction. Given its role in inflammatory processes, PTAFR may be implicated in the pathophysiology of Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH), where inflammation and vascular compromise are critical factors in disease progression. However, the retrieved evidence does not directly link PTAFR to SONFH, indicating a lack of specific studies connecting this receptor to the disease context. Therefore, while PTAFR's involvement in inflammatory pathways suggests a potential role in SONFH, the evidence is primarily inferred rather than directly supported by specific studies. As such, its clinical relevance as a biomarker for early detection of SONFH remains weak and speculative.
relevance_summary: PTAFR may be involved in inflammatory processes relevant to Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH), but lacks direct evidence linking it to the disease.

result.title: PTAFR (P25105)
result.text:
Protein: Platelet-activating factor receptor
Function: Receptor for platelet activating factor, a chemotactic phospholipid mediator that possesses potent inflammatory, smooth-muscle contractile and hypotensive activity. Seems to mediate its action via a G protein that activates a phosphatidylinositol-calcium second messenger system

result.title: PTAFR — PTAFR
result.text: Function: Receptor for platelet activating factor, a chemotactic phospholipid mediator that possesses potent inflammatory, smooth-muscle contractile and hypotensive activity. Seems to mediate its action via a G protein that activates a phosphatidylinositol-calcium second messenger system.

result.title: PTAFR ↔ allergic disease
result.text:
Disease: allergic disease
Description: An immune response that occurs following re-exposure to an innocuous antigen, and that requires the presence of existing antibodies against that antigen. This response involves the binding of IgE to mast cells, and may worsen with repeated exposures.
Association score: 0.462

result.title: PTAFR ↔ urticaria
result.text:
Disease: urticaria
Description: A vascular reaction of the skin characterized by erythema and wheal formation due to localized increase of vascular permeability. The causative mechanism may be allergy, infection, or stress.
Association score: 0.332

result.title: PTAFR ↔ rheumatoid arthritis
result.text:
Disease: rheumatoid arthritis
Description: A chronic, systemic autoimmune disorder characterized by inflammation in the synovial membranes and articular surfaces. It manifests primarily as a symmetric, erosive polyarthritis that spares the axial skeleton and is typically associated with the presence in the serum of rheumatoid factor.
Association score: 0.259

result.title: PTAFR ↔ Mastocytosis
result.text:
Disease: Mastocytosis
Description: A clonal myeloproliferative neoplasm characterized by the proliferation and accumulation of neoplastic mast cells in one or multiple organs or organ systems. It is a heterogeneous group of neoplasms, ranging from cutaneous proliferations which may regress spontaneously, to aggressive neoplasms associated with organ failure and short survival.
Association score: 0.185

result.title: Platelet-activating factor receptor
result.text:
The platelet-activating factor receptor (PAF-R) is a G-protein coupled receptor which binds platelet-activating factor. It is encoded in the human by the PTAFR gene.
The PAF receptor shows structural characteristics of the rhodopsin (MIM 180380) gene family and binds platelet-activating factor (PAF). PAF is a phospholipid (1-0-alkyl-2-acetyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphorylcholine) that has been implicated as a mediator in diverse pathologic processes, such as allergy, asthma, septic shock, arterial thrombosis, and inflammatory processes.[supplied by OMIM] Its pathogenetic role in chronic kidney failure has also been reported recently.

Ligands
Agonists

Platelet activating factor
Antagonists

Apafant (WEB-2086)
Israpafant (Y-24180)
Lexipafant
Rupatadine
